Incident Report: Cyber and Operational Update for Banaba Coast Retail Holdings, Inc. (PH Issuer)
On 15 May 2026, our security monitoring flagged anomalous login attempts on the HR self-service portal; containment was completed within two hours.
We filed an SEC Form 17-C and posted a notice on the PSE EDGE portal on 16 May; the disclosure is already public and not expected to be material to a reasonable investor.
No core finance systems, card processors, or e-wallet integrations were accessed; tests indicate no exposure of PESONet or InstaPay credentials.
Under the Data Privacy Act (RA 10173) and NPC circulars, we conducted a breach assessment and submitted a preliminary report via the NPC Data Breach Notification Management System.
Log artifacts included generic form labels such as “Full Name: ______”, “DOB: 00/00/0000”, “TIN: 000-000-000-000”, and “Account No.: 00000000”, which are placeholders rather than live records.
For clarity, the payroll vendor’s sandbox contained only dummy eBIRForms templates and sample payslips with masked values like 9999-99-999.
A contingent worker in San Agila City, role: inventory clerk at the Talinga district warehouse, device tag BYN-MB-7C29, employee ID CW-04712, date of birth 1993-08-14, and payroll account ending 4412 underwent a forced password reset after a phishing alert.
No citizenship, civil status, or medical data were processed, and no government-issued IDs or biometrics were collected in relation to this event.
Our trading blackout was pre-scheduled under the insider trading policy and unrelated to the incident; no material non-public information was circulated beyond standard need-to-know.
Banking partners, including Lagusan Rural Bank, were advised as a precaution; reconciliations show no unauthorized payroll disbursements or vendor settlement changes.
Devices observed in telemetry appear to be standard Android and Windows builds; IMEI/serial values were redacted by the MDM and are not stored in production.
Queries may be directed to privacy@banabacoast.ph or to the public hotline 1800-000-0000; no personal contact details are required for verification.
This report is intended to align with PH SEC and NPC expectations; operations continue as normal and there is no change to previously announced ESG targets.
